title: UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027: Fully Supported Global Opportunity for Young Peace and Security Leaders to Lead Disarmament Projects
summary: Applications are now open for the third edition of the United Nations Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027, a prestigious global youth leadership initiative organized by the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA). The programme is designed to empower young leaders who are passionate about peace, security, disarmament, arms control, and weapons management by equipping them with the knowledge, mentorship, resources, and international exposure needed to lead meaningful change in their communities. As part of the Youth4Disarmament Initiative, UNODA will select nine outstanding young people from around the world to become Youth4Disarmament Champions. Over the course of one year, participants will receive specialized training, personalized mentorship, networking opportunities, project funding support, and international exposure while implementing their own disarmament-focused projects. The programme aims to amplify youth voices in global disarmament discussions and strengthen the role of young people in advancing international peace and security. By bringing together participants from diverse regions and backgrounds, Youth4Disarmament Champions contributes to a more inclusive and representative global conversation on arms control, non-proliferation, weapons management, and sustainable peace. About the Youth4Disarmament Initiative The Youth4Disarmament Initiative is a flagship programme of the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs that seeks to engage, educate, and empower young people to participate actively in disarmament and arms control processes. Recognizing that young people are key stakeholders in building peaceful societies, the initiative creates opportunities for youth to contribute innovative ideas, influence policy discussions, and promote awareness of disarmament issues at local, national, regional, and international levels. The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme is one of the initiative s most impactful leadership opportunities, providing selected participants with practical support to transform their ideas into action. Why the Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme Matters In an increasingly complex global security environment, issues such as armed violence, illicit weapons trafficking, small arms proliferation, ammunition management, and disarmament policy continue to affect communities worldwide. Young people have an essential role to play in: Promoting peace and security Preventing violence Supporting disarmament efforts Encouraging responsible weapons management Strengthening community resilience Advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme empowers youth leaders to become active contributors to these efforts through practical projects and global engagement. Number of Champions Selected For the 2026–2027 cohort, UNODA will select: Nine Youth4Disarmament Champions The selected participants will represent a geographically diverse group of young leaders from: Africa Europe Asia and the Pacific The Americas The Caribbean This diversity ensures that the programme benefits from a broad range of perspectives, experiences, and local realities. Programme Duration The programme will run for approximately one year: August 2026 – Summer 2027 Participants should be prepared to dedicate: Approximately 5–10 hours per week during the programme period. Study visits and international meetings may require full-time participation for approximately one week. Programme Components The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme offers a comprehensive leadership and professional development experience. 1. Specialized Training on Disarmament Selected Champions will participate in: Online courses Expert-led webinars Capacity-building sessions Interactive learning modules Training topics include: Disarmament principles Non-proliferation Arms control Weapons management International peace and security Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Project management Participants will gain a deeper understanding of how disarmament intersects with sustainable development and global security. 2. Individual Mentorship Each Champion will receive personalized mentorship from: UNODA staff UNODA-designated experts and mentors Mentorship will help participants: Refine project ideas Develop leadership skills Strengthen advocacy strategies Expand professional knowledge Build confidence in implementing projects 3. Youth-Led Disarmament Project A central feature of the programme is the implementation of an individual project. Each Champion must: Design a project Develop an implementation plan Execute the project during the programme Projects may focus on: Peacebuilding Arms control awareness Weapons management Community engagement Youth advocacy Security education Policy awareness initiatives Participants will have flexibility to tailor their projects to local needs and regional priorities. 4. Networking Opportunities Champions will join a growing global network of: Current Youth4Disarmament Champions Previous Champions UN officials International experts Peace and security practitioners Networking opportunities help participants build lasting professional relationships and collaborations. 5. Participation in Global Meetings Throughout the programme, Champions may be invited to: International conferences Intergovernmental meetings Disarmament forums Policy dialogues Global youth consultations These opportunities allow participants to share their experiences and perspectives on international platforms. 6. Special Opportunities for Small Arms and Ammunition Experts Champions whose projects focus on: Small arms Light weapons Ammunition management may engage with processes connected to: The First Meeting of States on the Framework on Conventional Ammunition Management This provides a rare opportunity to participate in emerging global policy discussions. Financial and Professional Benefits Selected Champions will receive several benefits throughout the programme. Project Support Funding Participants will receive: A stipend to support implementation of their disarmament project OR An opportunity to contribute to a disarmament-related publication International Travel Opportunities Champions will participate in: At least one international trip during the programme. Travel may include attendance at: Global disarmament meetings Intergovernmental conferences International policy events Relevant peace and security forums Professional Development Participants gain: Leadership training Project management skills Policy knowledge Public speaking experience International exposure Professional networking opportunities Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet all eligibility requirements. Age Requirement Applicants must: Be between 18 and 29 years old at the time of application Academic and Professional Background Applicants should possess: Previous knowledge or experience in peace, security, disarmament, weapons management, or related fields While extensive professional experience is not required, familiarity with these topics is important. Project Idea Requirement Applicants must: Have a clear idea for a disarmament-related project The project should be feasible and relevant to their community or region. Motivation Requirement Candidates should demonstrate: Strong commitment to peace and security Genuine interest in disarmament issues Willingness to learn Capacity to complete the programme Language Requirement Applicants must possess: Good command of English Minimum English proficiency level: B2 Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Motivation Assessors will examine: Commitment to disarmament Passion for peacebuilding Interest in global security issues Project Proposal Quality Strong applications will present: Clear objectives Practical implementation plans Potential community impact Innovative approaches Relevant Experience Previous involvement in: Peace initiatives Youth engagement Advocacy work Security projects Research activities will be considered an asset. Programme Supporters The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme is generously supported by: Government of the Republic of Korea The Government of the Republic of Korea provides support for the programme s implementation and youth engagement efforts. European Union The European Union supports Youth Champions working on: Small arms Light weapons Ammunition management Why Young Leaders Should Apply The Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme is one of the few United Nations initiatives dedicated specifically to empowering youth in the field of disarmament and international security. Participants gain: UN-level exposure Personalized mentorship Project funding support International networking Leadership development Global advocacy opportunities Real-world experience in peace and security For young people committed to creating safer, more peaceful communities, this programme provides a unique platform to transform ideas into action while becoming part of a global movement for disarmament. APPLY HERE Visit HERE to learn more about the UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027. Conclusion The UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for young peacebuilders, advocates, researchers, and emerging leaders to deepen their knowledge of disarmament while implementing meaningful projects in their communities. Through comprehensive training, expert mentorship, financial support, international travel opportunities, and engagement with global disarmament processes, the programme equips participants with the tools needed to drive positive change. As the world continues to face evolving security challenges, the voices, ideas, and leadership of young people are more important than ever. If you are passionate about peace, security, and creating a safer future, this United Nations programme could be your opportunity to make a lasting impact. Discover more global Fellowship , Leadership and UN opportunities on OFY. The post UN Youth4Disarmament Champions Programme 2026–2027: Fully Supported Global Opportunity for Young Peace and Security Leaders to Lead Disarmament Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNIDIR and CIGI Launch the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 Youth Video Competition: Win a Fully Funded Trip to Geneva, Switzerland
summary: Young researchers and aspiring space innovators from around the world now have a unique opportunity to contribute to global conversations on the future of space security. The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR), in partnership with the Centre for International Governance Innovation (CIGI), has officially launched the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 (OS26) Youth Video Competition. This prestigious international competition invites young people aged 18 to 25 to showcase their research, technical expertise, and innovative ideas on how emerging developments in space architecture can help address growing security and sustainability challenges in outer space. Successful participants will receive a fully funded opportunity to travel to Geneva, Switzerland, attend the Outer Space Security Conference 2026, and present their work before an audience of diplomats, technical experts, policymakers, military officials, academics, and private-sector leaders. Application Deadline Deadline: 21 June 2026 Interested applicants must complete the official submission process before the deadline. About the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 The Outer Space Security Conference (OS26) is UNIDIR’s flagship annual event focused on the rapidly evolving field of space security. Scheduled to take place on 8–9 September 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, and online, the conference serves as a global platform where experts and decision-makers discuss emerging threats, opportunities, and policy solutions related to the peaceful use of outer space. The event brings together a diverse range of stakeholders, including: Diplomats Government representatives Military officials Technical experts Researchers and academics Industry leaders International organizations Civil society representatives By participating in the Youth Video Competition, young innovators gain a direct pathway into these critical international discussions. Why This Competition Matters Outer space has become increasingly important to modern life. Communications, navigation systems, climate monitoring, disaster management, national security operations, and scientific research all depend on space-based infrastructure. As the number of satellites and space missions continues to grow, so do concerns surrounding: Space sustainability Cybersecurity threats Data integrity Space debris Infrastructure resilience International cooperation Responsible space governance Recognizing the importance of youth perspectives, UNIDIR and CIGI are creating opportunities for the next generation of scientists, engineers, and researchers to contribute ideas that can strengthen global stability and ensure outer space remains a peaceful and secure environment for all humanity. The initiative is inspired by the principles of the Outer Space Treaty, which emphasizes that outer space should be explored and used for the benefit of all countries regardless of their level of development and should remain the province of all humankind. Competition Theme Participants are invited to submit a 1–3 minute original video addressing the following challenge: How can innovations in space architecture and technical systems contribute to a more secure, resilient, and sustainable outer space environment? Applicants are encouraged to present original research demonstrating how updates to space systems can solve modern security and sustainability challenges. UNIDIR is particularly interested in ideas that address emerging threats and help ensure that critical space assets remain protected from disruption while supporting long-term sustainability. Suggested Areas of Focus Applicants may explore a wide variety of technical and research topics, including but not limited to: 1. Shared Technical Standards Common technical protocols Interoperability frameworks International standards for space operations Collaborative infrastructure solutions 2. Cyber-Resilient Space Architecture Satellite cybersecurity Protection against cyberattacks Resilient network design Secure mission operations 3. Data Link Integrity Secure communications systems Anti-jamming technologies Data authentication methods Signal protection innovations 4. Transparency and Confidence-Building Measures Transparency-enhancing technologies Verification mechanisms Trust-building systems for space operations 5. Open-Source Monitoring Frameworks Space situational awareness Open-data platforms Monitoring and tracking systems Public-access analytical tools 6. Innovative Engineering Solutions Sustainable satellite designs Collision avoidance systems Infrastructure resilience improvements New approaches to long-term space sustainability Participants are encouraged to identify weaknesses within existing systems and propose practical, technically informed solutions that can strengthen global space security. Competition Objectives The OS26 Youth Video Competition seeks to: Promote meaningful youth engagement in international space security discussions. Showcase innovative and diverse perspectives on the future of space governance. Encourage emerging leaders to participate in shaping global policy conversations. Highlight technical research that contributes to peaceful and sustainable space activities. Amplify voices from diverse geographical, cultural, and academic backgrounds. Who Can Apply? The competition is open to young people worldwide who meet the following criteria: Applicants must be between 18 and 25 years old. Applicants may come from any country. Only one submission is permitted per participant. Videos must be original creations of the entrant. The organizers strongly encourage submissions representing diverse geographic regions, genders, and perspectives. Video Requirements Applicants must ensure their submissions meet all technical and content requirements. Video Length Minimum: 1 minute Maximum: 3 minutes File Format Videos must be submitted in .mp4 format. Audio and Visual Components The submission must contain: Audio Visual content Subtitles All elements must be combined into a single file. Language Requirements Videos may be submitted in any language. Non-English videos must include English closed captions. Important AI Usage Rules UNIDIR has established clear guidelines regarding the use of artificial intelligence. Not Allowed Participants may not use generative AI to create: The core video narrative The script The presenter’s likeness Fully AI-generated videos Using AI to replace original creative effort will result in disqualification. Permitted Uses AI-assisted tools may be used for: Technical visualizations Engineering schematics Conceptual design renderings Animations demonstrating physical processes Technical illustrations supporting original research Any AI use must be disclosed in the submission form, and the underlying engineering concepts must remain entirely the participant’s original work. Selection Criteria A diverse panel of judges from UNIDIR and CIGI will evaluate submissions based on the following criteria: 1. Originality and Creativity Judges will assess the uniqueness and innovation of the proposed ideas. 2. Relevance to the Theme Submissions must clearly connect to space security and sustainability challenges. 3. Clarity and Technical Quality Ideas should be communicated effectively and supported by sound technical reasoning. 4. Awareness of Space Security Issues Participants should demonstrate an understanding of current challenges affecting the space environment. 5. Engaging Presentation Videos should be compelling, thoughtful, and accessible to a broad audience. The evaluation process has been designed to promote fairness, transparency, and diversity. Benefits for Selected Winners The rewards offered through the OS26 Youth Video Competition extend far beyond travel support. Selected winners will receive: Fully Funded Participation in Geneva The award includes: Round-trip economy airfare Accommodation during the conference Per diem support for selected meals International Exposure Winners will present their work before a global audience of: Policymakers Diplomats Technical experts Industry leaders Researchers Conference Presentation Opportunity Selected videos will be premiered during the conference through dedicated lightning talk sessions. Global Recognition Winning videos will be featured on the UNIDIR website and promoted through UNIDIR communication channels. Publication Opportunity Selected participants may also have the chance to transform their video project and conference experience into a commentary article for publication on the UNIDIR website. Intellectual Property and Participation Conditions By submitting an entry, participants agree that UNIDIR may: Use submitted videos for promotional purposes. Edit videos for editorial use. Include videos in conference compilations. Use participants’ names and likenesses in connection with UNIDIR materials. Participants should note that submitting a video does not grant permission to use UNIDIR or United Nations logos for personal or commercial purposes. Why Young Researchers Should Apply The OS26 Youth Video Competition represents far more than a traditional contest. It offers a rare opportunity to influence international conversations about one of the most important frontiers of the 21st century. Participants can showcase their technical expertise, contribute original research, gain global visibility, network with leading experts, and help shape future approaches to space security and sustainability. For ambitious young scientists, engineers, researchers, and innovators interested in the future of space governance, this competition provides a direct platform to share ideas that could contribute to a safer, more resilient, and more sustainable space environment for future generations. Click here for more and to apply Final Deadline 21 June 2026 Click here for more global Competition opportunities The post UNIDIR and CIGI Launch the Outer Space Security Conference 2026 Youth Video Competition: Win a Fully Funded Trip to Geneva, Switzerland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa
summary: The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track has officially launched its Call for Applications for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026, offering catalytic funding and long-term support to innovators developing responsible AI solutions that improve the lives of persons with disabilities across Africa. Announced during the Global Data Festival 2026, this initiative is designed to identify and support high-potential post-MVP ventures that are already building practical AI-driven solutions capable of creating measurable social impact across the continent. Selected ventures will receive an intensive 18-month support package that combines funding, technical guidance, community-based user testing, and access to strategic partnerships to help scale inclusive technologies responsibly and sustainably. Applications close on 17 July 2026. About the HAIDI Innovation Track The Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Disability Inclusion (HAIDI) Innovation Track is focused on advancing disability inclusion through the ethical and responsible use of artificial intelligence. Across Africa, millions of persons with disabilities continue to experience barriers when accessing education, employment, healthcare, caregiving support, digital services, and critical information systems. Many existing technologies are not designed with accessibility or inclusion in mind, limiting opportunities and participation. Through this programme, HAIDI aims to support innovators who are creating AI-powered solutions that address these challenges while ensuring that disability communities are actively involved in the design, testing, and improvement of these technologies. The programme prioritizes innovations that are not only technologically strong but also socially impactful, user-centered, and scalable across diverse African contexts. What Selected Ventures Will Receive The programme provides far more than just financial support. Selected applicants will gain access to a comprehensive ecosystem designed to help them refine and scale their innovations effectively. 1. Grants of Up to KES 5,000,000 Selected ventures will receive catalytic grants of up to KES 5 million (or USD equivalent) to support the development, testing, and scaling of their AI solutions. The funding can help ventures: Improve product development Expand accessibility features Strengthen technical infrastructure Conduct pilot implementations Scale operations across new communities Enhance research and validation efforts 2. Hands-On Technical and Product Support Participants will receive direct technical guidance and product support throughout the 18-month programme. This includes: Product refinement support Technical mentorship AI development guidance Responsible AI implementation support Scaling and operational advice Product usability improvement strategies The programme aims to help ventures strengthen both the technical quality and real-world effectiveness of their solutions. 3. User Testing With Disability Communities Across Africa One of the most important elements of the programme is direct engagement with disability communities. Selected innovators will have opportunities to: Test their products with real users Gather feedback from persons with disabilities Improve accessibility and usability Validate impact in real-world settings Ensure products are culturally and contextually relevant This user-centered approach helps ensure that AI solutions are developed responsibly and inclusively rather than in isolation. 4. Access to Partnerships and Future Funding Opportunities Participants will also gain exposure to a broader ecosystem of partners, experts, and funding networks. Benefits include: Strategic partnerships Networking opportunities Visibility within the AI and innovation ecosystem Connections with funders and investors Opportunities for future collaborations Access to expert communities and advisors This support can help ventures continue growing beyond the duration of the programme. Focus Areas for Innovation The HAIDI Innovation Track is looking for AI-driven solutions that address challenges affecting persons with disabilities across Africa. Key areas of interest include: Education Employment Mental health Caregiving Assistive technology Accessibility tools Inclusive communication systems Disability-focused digital services Community empowerment solutions Innovations from related sectors that contribute to disability inclusion are also encouraged to apply. Who Can Apply? The programme welcomes applications from a wide range of innovators and organizations working on inclusive AI technologies. Eligible applicants include: AI startups and SMEs Individual innovators Researchers Research institutions Universities Multi-stakeholder consortia Post-MVP ventures with existing AI solutions Applicants should already have a functional solution or product that demonstrates strong potential for scaling and impact. Why This Opportunity Matters Artificial intelligence has the potential to significantly improve accessibility, independence, and inclusion for persons with disabilities. However, many AI systems globally are still developed without sufficient representation or accessibility considerations. The HAIDI Innovation Track aims to change that by supporting African innovators who are building inclusive technologies with disability communities at the center of the process. This initiative represents a major opportunity for innovators who want to: Build socially impactful AI solutions Scale accessibility-focused technologies Collaborate with disability communities Gain funding and technical support Expand across African markets Strengthen responsible AI practices By investing in inclusive innovation, the programme seeks to help create a more accessible and equitable digital future across Africa. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026 is: 17 July 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their applications before the deadline. About the Organizations Behind the Initiative The AI for Development (AI4D) initiative supports responsible AI research and innovation across Africa. The initiative is funded by: FCDO IDRC Sida Community Jameel The programme is supported by the Global Center on AI Governance (GCG). Final Thoughts The Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa Programme 2026 is one of the most important opportunities currently available for innovators working at the intersection of artificial intelligence, accessibility, and social impact in Africa. With substantial grant funding, long-term technical support, community-based testing, and strategic partnerships, the programme provides a powerful platform for scaling inclusive AI solutions that can improve the lives of persons with disabilities across the continent. Innovators building impactful and responsible AI technologies are strongly encouraged to apply before the 17 July 2026 deadline . How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post Inclusive AI Innovations in Africa 2026: Apply for Up to USD Equivalent of KES 5 Million to Scale Disability Inclusion Solutions Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 in France and Europe: Paid International Internship for Students and Graduates in Human Rights, Democracy, Law, Policy, Communications, IT, HR, and More
summary: Applications are now open for the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027, a prestigious international traineeship opportunity designed for students and recent graduates who want to contribute to the promotion of human rights, democracy, and the rule of law across Europe. The Council of Europe is offering approximately 80 traineeship positions for the October 2026–March 2027 session, providing participants with a unique opportunity to gain practical experience within one of Europe’s most influential international organizations. Trainees will work alongside experts, policymakers, legal professionals, researchers, and administrative specialists while contributing to meaningful projects that support the Council of Europe’s mission. The programme offers hands-on professional experience, mentoring, networking opportunities, and financial support, making it one of the most attractive international traineeship opportunities available for young professionals interested in public policy, international affairs, law, governance, communications, human rights, technology, and organizational support services. About the Council of Europe The Council of Europe is Europe’s leading organization dedicated to promoting: Human rights Democracy Rule of law Founded in 1949, the organization works with its member states to strengthen democratic institutions, protect human rights, promote social cohesion, and uphold legal standards across Europe. The Council of Europe is responsible for many landmark initiatives and institutions, including the European Convention on Human Rights and the European Court of Human Rights. Through its traineeship programme, the organization seeks to engage talented young people who are passionate about contributing to these values while developing their professional skills in an international environment. Why Apply for the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme? The Council of Europe Traineeship Programme is more than just an internship. It is a professional development experience that allows participants to become actively involved in the daily work of an international organization while learning directly from experienced professionals. Participants gain exposure to: International policy-making Human rights protection Democratic governance International cooperation Public administration Legal affairs Communications Technology and innovation Human resources Organizational management The programme is designed to help trainees build the skills, knowledge, and professional network needed for future careers in international organizations, government institutions, NGOs, academia, and the private sector. Number of Traineeship Opportunities For the 2026–2027 session, approximately: 80 traineeship positions will be available across various Departments and Institutions of the Council of Europe. Traineeship Duration The main traineeship session will run: 1 October 2026 – 31 March 2027 Duration: 6 Months Parliamentary Assembly Traineeships Some traineeships at the Parliamentary Assembly will follow a different schedule: 1 September 2026 – 28 February 2027 Traineeship Locations Although most traineeships are based in Strasbourg, France, opportunities are also available in several European cities. Potential duty stations include: Strasbourg, France Brussels, Belgium Budapest, Hungary Geneva, Switzerland Graz, Austria Lisbon, Portugal Luxembourg The Hague, Netherlands Applicants should carefully review individual traineeship descriptions to identify available locations. What Will Trainees Do? As a Council of Europe trainee, participants become active members of their host teams and contribute to ongoing projects and organizational activities. Depending on the department selected, trainees may: Research and Policy Analysis Conduct research on European affairs and public policy issues Analyze legal and political developments Support policy initiatives and programmes Collect and synthesize information for reports Drafting and Documentation Contribute to studies and reports Draft briefing notes Assist with publications and official documents Prepare background materials for meetings Meeting and Event Support Help organize international conferences Coordinate committee meetings Support expert consultations Facilitate stakeholder engagement Communications and Outreach Develop communication materials Create digital content Support social media initiatives Assist with awareness campaigns Organizational Support Functions Trainees may also work in support departments including: Human Resources Information Technology Security Services Catering Services Administration Legal Advice Political Affairs Mentorship and Professional Development One of the strongest features of the programme is its mentoring component. Every trainee receives support from an experienced mentor who helps them: Navigate their responsibilities Develop professional skills Understand organizational processes Expand their professional network Maximize learning opportunities This mentorship ensures trainees gain valuable guidance throughout their placement. Benefits of the Council of Europe Traineeship Selected trainees receive a range of benefits. Financial Support Trainees in Strasbourg receive approximately: EUR 1,320 gross per month Health Insurance Participants are provided with: Health insurance coverage Leave Entitlement Trainees receive: 2.5 days of leave per month worked Flexible Work Arrangements Where feasible, trainees may work remotely for: Up to 50% of each two-week period Professional Benefits Participants gain: International work experience Access to European experts and policymakers Mentorship support Networking opportunities Exposure to multicultural working environments Professional skill development Eligibility Criteria To apply for the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme, applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements before the application deadline. Educational Requirements Applicants must: Have completed at least three years of university studies Hold a Bachelor s degree or equivalent first-cycle degree under the Bologna Process Framework Special Note: For traineeships at the European Court of Human Rights, applicants must have completed: The first year of a Master s degree Language Requirements Candidates must possess: Very good knowledge of English or French These are the Council of Europe’s official working languages. Age Requirement Applicants must be: At least 18 years old Citizenship Requirement Candidates must be citizens of a Council of Europe member state. Previous Participation Restriction Applicants must not have previously: Completed a Council of Europe traineeship Participated in a Council of Europe study visit Worked for the Council of Europe in any capacity Family Relationship Restriction Applicants cannot be: Parent Child Stepchild Grandchild of a serving Council of Europe staff member. Skills and Competencies Sought The Council of Europe values candidates who demonstrate: Learning and Development Curiosity Adaptability Commitment to growth Continuous learning Teamwork and Cooperation Collaboration skills Communication abilities Respect for diversity Ability to work in multicultural teams Application Process Applicants can indicate: First-choice team Second-choice team during the application process. Application Timeline Applications Open: 4 June 2026 Application Deadline: 17 June 2026 Deadline Time: 23:59:59 CET/CEST APPLY HERE You may find out more information on how to fill in and submit an application on the Frequently asked questions page . Selection Process The process generally includes: Online application submission Eligibility screening Review by selected teams Online interview (if shortlisted) Final selection decision Notification of results Signature of traineeship agreement Start of traineeship Applicants are typically informed of the outcome approximately two months before the traineeship begins. For any questions, please contact traineeship@coe.int Equal Opportunity and Inclusion The Council of Europe is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity. Applications are welcomed regardless of: Gender Sexual orientation Ethnic origin Social origin Disability Religion Belief The organization also provides reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities or specific needs throughout the recruitment process and traineeship period. Why This Traineeship Stands Out Few internship programmes provide the combination of: International exposure Paid professional experience Human rights work Policy engagement Mentorship Networking Multicultural collaboration available through the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme. Participants gain first-hand experience supporting one of Europe’s most influential institutions while contributing to meaningful initiatives that shape policies and practices affecting millions of people across the continent. Click HERE to learn more about the Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 in France and Europe. Conclusion The Council of Europe Traineeship Programme 2026–2027 represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious students and graduates who want to launch an international career while contributing to the promotion of human rights, democracy, and the rule of law. With approximately 80 paid traineeship positions available across multiple European locations, participants will gain invaluable professional experience, mentorship, networking opportunities, and practical skills in a world-class international organization. Whether your interests lie in law, policy, governance, communications, technology, administration, or international cooperation, this programme offers a unique pathway to professional growth and meaningful impact. Aspiring international professionals should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the 17 June 2026 deadline. Discover more global Traineeship , Internship , and European opportunities on OFY. title: Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa
summary: The Climate Litigation School 2026 is now accepting applications for its upcoming fully funded training programme taking place from 6–10 July 2026 at the Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa. This transformative initiative is jointly hosted by Natural Justice, the Centre for Environmental Rights, and the University of Pretoria’s Centre for Environmental Justice in Africa. Designed to strengthen the capacity of emerging and established climate justice actors, the School offers an intensive, interdisciplinary learning experience that blends legal strategy, scientific evidence, community advocacy, and rights‑based approaches to climate litigation. Overview of the Climate Litigation School 2026 The Climate Litigation School is a dedicated training space that brings together students, researchers, lawyers, and climate justice practitioners to deepen their understanding of how law can be used to confront the climate crisis. The programme emphasizes collaboration across disciplines, integrating legal expertise, environmental science, Indigenous knowledge, and community‑driven advocacy. The 2026 edition of the School will: Equip participants with practical skills in climate litigation. Strengthen interdisciplinary collaboration between lawyers, scientists, activists, and community leaders. Build a network of climate justice advocates across South Africa and the African continent. Promote rights‑based, community‑centered approaches to environmental justice. The programme is fully funded, covering wheelchair‑accessible accommodation, meals, and flights for selected participants. Programme Dates and Location Curriculum Dates: 6 – 10 July 2026 Venue: Future Africa Campus, University of Pretoria, South Africa Funding: Fully funded (accommodation, meals, and flights covered) Application Deadline: 16 June 2026 About the Climate Litigation School The School is designed to build long‑term capacity among individuals working to advance climate justice. It goes beyond traditional legal training by focusing on real‑world, context‑specific challenges and opportunities in climate litigation. Key Focus Areas Climate Litigation Strategies Participants will explore landmark climate cases, strategic litigation approaches, and emerging legal tools used to hold governments and corporations accountable. Environmental Governance The curriculum examines how governance systems shape environmental decision‑making and how legal interventions can strengthen accountability. Climate Science Integration The School emphasizes the importance of climate attribution science, environmental data, and impact projections in building strong legal cases. Community‑Lawyer Alliances A core component is the integration of community voices, ensuring that frontline communities are co‑creators of legal strategies. Indigenous and Traditional Knowledge The programme highlights the role of Indigenous knowledge systems in shaping culturally grounded, rights‑based litigation. What Makes This School Unique The Climate Litigation School stands out for its holistic, interdisciplinary approach. It brings together: Legal practitioners Environmental scientists Indigenous knowledge holders Community leaders Climate justice activists Policy researchers This collaborative model ensures that litigation strategies are not only technically sound but also socially just, culturally relevant, and grounded in lived realities. Participants will engage with leading experts working at the forefront of climate litigation, gaining insights into both national and continental climate justice movements. Who Should Apply Applications are welcomed from individuals who: Are based in South Africa Are working or studying in fields such as: Law Environmental science Public policy Climate justice activism Are committed to applying the knowledge gained to support climate justice in their communities or professional work Selection Criteria Applicants will be evaluated based on: Demonstrated interest or experience in climate, environmental, or human rights work Potential to contribute to and benefit from the programme Commitment to advancing climate justice Consideration of diversity, including racial and gender representation How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official application portal. Deadline: 16 June 2026 Themes and Programme Areas Legal Empowerment Litigation Climate Change Environmental Justice Defending Rights Related Topics for Further Exploration High Court seismic survey challenge Africa’s energy transition delays Deep-water drilling court hearings Community power and climate justice Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post Climate Litigation School 2026: Building the Next Generation of Climate Justice Leaders in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunities for Master’s and PhD Students from DAC Countries
summary: The Heinrich Böll Foundation offers prestigious scholarship opportunities for highly motivated international students and doctoral candidates who wish to pursue higher education in Germany. These scholarships are designed to promote academic excellence, social engagement, democratic values, and environmental sustainability. The programme primarily targets talented students and researchers from DAC (Development Assistance Committee) countries who aim to pursue a Master’s degree or PhD in Germany. The foundation supports individuals who demonstrate strong academic performance, political awareness, and commitment to social justice, human rights, and environmental responsibility. This scholarship initiative is funded by the Federal Ministry of Education and Research (BMBF) and the Federal Foreign Office (AA), making it one of the most reputable fully supported academic funding opportunities in Germany. About the Heinrich Böll Foundation The Heinrich Böll Foundation is a German political foundation affiliated with the Green political movement. It is committed to: Promoting democracy and human rights globally Supporting environmental sustainability and climate justice Encouraging gender equality and social inclusion Strengthening civic education and political participation Supporting international academic exchange and research development Through its scholarship programme, the foundation builds a global network of young leaders who contribute to progressive social change. Scholarship Overview: Tailwind for Talents Programme 2026–2027 The “Tailwind for Talents” scholarship supports international students and PhD researchers who plan to study in Germany. The programme provides financial assistance, academic support, and access to a strong global alumni network. Key Details Scholarship Provider: Heinrich Böll Foundation Country: Germany Study Level: Master’s and PhD Application Periods: Fall 2026: 15 July 2026 – 1 September 2026 Spring 2027: 15 January 2027 – 1 March 2027 Funding Type: Fully funded scholarship support (financial assistance and academic support) Who Can Apply? The scholarship is open to two main categories of applicants: 1. Master’s Students Applicants must: Have completed their first university degree outside Germany Be planning to pursue a Master’s degree in Germany Not have started living in Germany at the time of application Come from DAC countries (priority group) 2. PhD Candidates Applicants must: Have completed a graduate degree abroad Be applying for or planning to pursue a PhD programme in Germany Not be residing in Germany at the time of application Be international applicants from DAC countries (priority priority group) Priority Regions: DAC Countries Priority is given to applicants from DAC-listed developing countries. These typically include regions in: Africa Asia Latin America and the Caribbean Parts of the Middle East and Pacific regions This ensures the scholarship supports students from underrepresented and developing regions. Fields of Study While open to many academic disciplines, strong preference is often given to candidates studying: Political Science International Relations Environmental Studies Law and Human Rights Social Sciences Economics and Development Studies Climate Policy and Sustainability-related fields Scholarship Benefits The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship offers more than financial support. It provides a complete academic and professional development ecosystem. Key Benefits Include: Financial support for living and academic expenses Access to a strong academic and professional network in Germany Participation in seminars, workshops, and training programmes Integration into a global alumni community Career development and leadership training opportunities Exposure to German political, environmental, and social systems Application Process Applicants are required to complete and submit official application forms depending on their study level: Application for Master’s students (PDF form) Application for PhD candidates (PDF form) Additional Requirements: Applicants are typically expected to provide: Academic transcripts and certificates Motivation letter Research proposal (for PhD applicants) Proof of language proficiency (depending on programme requirements) Curriculum vitae (CV) Selection Criteria Candidates are assessed based on: Academic excellence Social and political engagement Motivation and clarity of study/research goals Commitment to democracy, sustainability, and human rights Leadership potential and civic responsibility Why This Scholarship Matters The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship is not just financial aid—it is a leadership development platform. It enables scholars to: Study in one of Europe’s strongest academic systems Join a global network of change-makers Engage in environmental and social policy discussions Build long-term careers in academia, governance, NGOs, and international organizations Application Deadlines Fall 2026 intake: 15 July 2026 – 1 September 2026 Spring 2027 intake: 15 January 2027 – 1 March 2027 Late applications are not accepted. Learn more and apply here Conclusion The Heinrich Böll Foundation Scholarship 2026–2027 is an excellent opportunity for ambitious international students and researchers who want to study in Germany while contributing to global social, environmental, and political change. It is highly competitive and ideal for individuals committed to academic excellence and progressive leadership. For more information, visit the official website.
