title: WFF Academic Engagement 2026 Applications Open: Connect Your Research to Global Food Policy and Agrifood Systems Transformation
summary: Universities, educators, researchers, and students around the world now have an exciting opportunity to engage directly with global policy discussions and contribute to the future of agrifood systems. The World Food Forum (WFF) Youth Initiative has officially opened applications for the 2026 WFF Academic Engagement Programme, inviting academic institutions and their communities to participate in one of the world s leading youth-driven platforms for food systems transformation. The programme offers a unique opportunity for students and faculty members to bridge the gap between academic research and real-world policy impact by engaging with global stakeholders, international experts, and policymakers working to address some of the most pressing challenges facing food systems today. Whether you are a student seeking to bring your research into international policy conversations, a faculty member interested in connecting classroom learning to practical global challenges, or a university aiming to strengthen its international presence and impact, the WFF Academic Engagement Programme provides a powerful platform for participation. Application Deadline Deadline: 24 June 2026 Interested institutions and participants should submit their applications before the closing date. What is the World Food Forum (WFF)? The World Food Forum (WFF) is a global youth-led movement and network designed to accelerate action toward transforming agrifood systems. Established as an initiative of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), the WFF serves as a platform where young people, researchers, innovators, policymakers, educators, and development practitioners come together to address global food security and sustainability challenges. The forum promotes collaboration, innovation, education, and policy engagement to create more sustainable, inclusive, resilient, and equitable agrifood systems worldwide. Through its various programmes and initiatives, the WFF empowers youth to contribute meaningful solutions to global challenges such as: Food insecurity Climate change Sustainable agriculture Rural development Agricultural innovation Nutrition and public health Food systems resilience Biodiversity conservation Sustainable development What is the WFF Academic Engagement Programme? The WFF Academic Engagement Programme is designed to connect higher education institutions with the World Food Forum s global activities and policy discussions. The programme enables students and educators to actively engage in international conversations surrounding agrifood systems while applying academic knowledge to real-world challenges. By participating, universities and academic communities gain opportunities to contribute research, share innovative ideas, and collaborate with global experts working on sustainable food systems transformation. The initiative strengthens the connection between academia and policy, ensuring that emerging research and youth perspectives help inform decision-making processes at local, national, and international levels. Why Academic Engagement Matters Universities play a critical role in generating knowledge, developing innovative solutions, and preparing future leaders. However, academic research often remains disconnected from policymaking processes and implementation efforts. The WFF Academic Engagement Programme addresses this gap by creating opportunities for students and faculty members to engage directly with global stakeholders and contribute to discussions that influence the future of food systems. Participation helps transform classroom learning into practical action while providing valuable international exposure. Participation Opportunities The 2026 WFF Academic Engagement Programme offers multiple pathways for involvement. 1. Academic Delegations Universities can form official academic delegations that participate in World Food Forum activities and events. Academic delegations provide opportunities for: Student participation in international discussions Faculty engagement with policymakers and experts Institutional representation at global events Networking with universities worldwide Knowledge exchange across disciplines and regions 2. Course-Integrated Participation Faculty members can integrate WFF activities into academic courses, allowing students to engage directly with real-world agrifood systems challenges as part of their learning experience. Benefits include: Experiential learning opportunities Applied research experiences Enhanced student engagement Global policy exposure Practical problem-solving exercises International collaboration opportunities Key Benefits of Participating The WFF Academic Engagement Programme offers substantial benefits for students, educators, and institutions alike. Benefits for Students Students who participate can: Engage with global policy consultations Connect academic research to real-world challenges Gain international exposure Build professional networks Interact with FAO experts and practitioners Strengthen leadership and communication skills Explore international career pathways Contribute to global food systems transformation Benefits for Faculty Members Educators and researchers can: Integrate global policy engagement into coursework Expand international collaborations Connect research outputs with policy discussions Engage with multidisciplinary experts Increase the visibility of academic work Enhance experiential learning opportunities for students Benefits for Universities Academic institutions can: Strengthen their global reputation Demonstrate commitment to sustainability and innovation Increase international visibility Expand institutional partnerships Showcase research excellence Position themselves as leaders in agrifood systems transformation Create new opportunities for students and faculty Opportunities to Engage with Global Policy Processes One of the programme s most valuable aspects is direct engagement with international policy conversations. Participants will have opportunities to: Engage with the WFF Youth Assembly The WFF Youth Assembly serves as a platform where young leaders discuss challenges and solutions related to food systems transformation. Participants can contribute perspectives, share research findings, and collaborate with peers from around the world. Participate in Global Policy Consultations The programme enables participants to engage with policy discussions addressing critical issues affecting agriculture, food security, sustainability, and rural development. This exposure helps students understand how research informs policy and decision-making processes. Collaborate with FAO Experts Participants gain access to technical experts from the Food and Agriculture Organization and other leading organizations working in agrifood systems. These interactions provide valuable insights into emerging challenges, best practices, and career opportunities. Building a Global Network The programme creates opportunities to join a diverse international community committed to advancing sustainable agrifood systems. Participants will connect with: Students from different countries Faculty members and educators Researchers and scholars Policymakers Development practitioners Industry leaders FAO technical experts Youth advocates and innovators These connections often lead to long-term collaborations, research partnerships, internships, and career opportunities. Recognition and Visibility Participation in the WFF Academic Engagement Programme can significantly enhance both individual and institutional visibility. Benefits may include: International recognition Showcasing academic projects and research Increased exposure to global audiences Opportunities to highlight innovative solutions Enhanced professional and institutional profiles For universities seeking to expand their global engagement, participation offers a valuable opportunity to demonstrate leadership in sustainability, innovation, and youth development. Why Universities Should Apply The future of food systems will be shaped by today s researchers, students, educators, and innovators. By participating in the 2026 WFF Academic Engagement Programme, universities can position themselves at the forefront of agrifood systems transformation while empowering their students and faculty to contribute to meaningful global change. The programme offers a unique combination of policy engagement, international collaboration, academic enrichment, and professional development that can generate lasting impact for participants and institutions alike. As food security, sustainability, and climate resilience continue to emerge as global priorities, engaging with platforms such as the World Food Forum allows academic institutions to play a central role in shaping solutions for future generations. Application Link Click here for more and to apply Final Deadline 24 June 2026 Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post WFF Academic Engagement 2026 Applications Open: Connect Your Research to Global Food Policy and Agrifood Systems Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific
summary: Applications are now open for the 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding , a prestigious leadership development programme offered by The Asia Foundation (TAF) for emerging peacebuilders from Asia and the Pacific. Designed to support the next generation of leaders working in peacebuilding, conflict transformation, mediation, advocacy, policy, and community engagement, the fellowship provides a year-long professional development experience that combines international exposure, leadership training, networking, and project support. Each year, the fellowship selects a small cohort of exceptional professionals who have demonstrated a commitment to advancing peace and addressing conflict challenges in their communities and countries. Through immersive learning experiences, engagement with global experts, and dedicated professional development funding, the fellowship equips participants with the knowledge, networks, and skills needed to strengthen their impact. For 2026, three fellows will be selected to participate in this highly competitive programme, which includes a two-week study tour in the United States, virtual leadership development seminars, and a professional development grant worth USD 2,500. About the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding The William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding is The Asia Foundation’s flagship leadership development initiative focused on peacebuilding and conflict transformation. Established in 2004 by The Asia Foundation’s Board of Trustees, the fellowship honors William P. Fuller, who served as President of The Asia Foundation for 15 years. The programme reflects his commitment to nurturing emerging leaders dedicated to peace, stability, and social cohesion throughout Asia and the Pacific. The fellowship is supported through privately endowed funds and seeks to empower professionals whose work contributes to building peaceful, inclusive, and resilient societies. As conflict dynamics continue to evolve throughout the region, there is increasing demand for leaders capable of: Bridging social, political, and cultural divides Facilitating dialogue among diverse stakeholders Promoting peaceful conflict resolution Designing locally grounded peacebuilding solutions Strengthening social cohesion Supporting reconciliation processes Addressing root causes of conflict The Fuller Fellowship aims to strengthen the leadership capacity of individuals already working in these areas. Why This Fellowship Matters The Asia-Pacific region continues to experience complex conflict and peacebuilding challenges, including: Political instability Social fragmentation Identity-based conflicts Community-level disputes Governance challenges Resource conflicts Emerging security concerns Addressing these issues requires innovative leaders who can combine research, policy engagement, community-based approaches, and collaborative problem-solving. The William P. Fuller Fellowship supports such leaders by helping them expand their influence, strengthen their networks, and gain access to global best practices in peacebuilding. What the Fellowship Offers The fellowship provides a comprehensive leadership development experience lasting one year. 1. Two-Week Study Tour in the United States One of the most valuable components of the fellowship is an intensive study tour and professional exchange programme. Selected fellows will travel to: San Francisco Bay Area New York City Washington, D.C. Study Tour Dates 10–21 November 2026 During the visit, fellows will engage with: Peacebuilding practitioners Policymakers Academic experts Civil society leaders Researchers Government representatives International development professionals The study tour provides exposure to diverse approaches to peacebuilding and conflict transformation from both local and global perspectives. 2. Leadership Development Seminars Fellows will participate in virtual learning sessions delivered through The Asia Foundation’s Leadership Exchange Programmes. Topics may include: Leadership development Peacebuilding strategies Conflict transformation Negotiation and mediation Policy engagement Systems thinking Strategic communication Stakeholder engagement 3. Professional Development Grant Each fellow receives: USD 2,500 Professional Development Grant The grant can be used to support: A self-designed project Professional learning goals Research initiatives Leadership development activities Community engagement projects Capacity-building efforts This funding enables fellows to pursue initiatives that strengthen their long-term impact. 4. International Professional Network Participants gain access to an influential network of: Peacebuilding experts Regional leaders Civil society practitioners Researchers Policymakers Alumni fellows This network often becomes a long-term source of collaboration and professional support. Fellowship Objectives The programme seeks to: Strengthen leadership skills among emerging peacebuilders. Expand professional networks across Asia and the Pacific. Support innovative approaches to conflict transformation. Encourage cross-border collaboration. Promote dialogue and mutual understanding. Advance sustainable peacebuilding efforts throughout the region. Develop future leaders capable of responding to evolving conflict challenges. Who Should Apply? The fellowship targets early- and mid-career professionals actively contributing to peacebuilding and conflict transformation. Applicants may be working in areas such as: Research Public policy Mediation Community engagement Peace advocacy Dialogue facilitation Human rights Conflict resolution Social cohesion Governance Development programming Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must be: 40 years old or younger during the programme year Born after 13 October 1986 Geographic Eligibility Applicants must come from a country where The Asia Foundation has a presence . U.S. Travel Eligibility Applicants must be able to secure a visa to travel to the United States. Special note: Candidates from: Myanmar Afghanistan Laos must currently be based in the United States. Language Requirement Applicants must be fluent in: Written English Spoken English Professional Experience Strong candidates typically have: At least three years of professional experience Demonstrated leadership capacity Experience advancing peacebuilding efforts through research or practice Commitment Requirement Applicants must be able to commit fully to all fellowship activities and programme dates. Characteristics of Strong Candidates The Asia Foundation is particularly interested in candidates who: Demonstrate leadership potential Are committed to peacebuilding in the Asia-Pacific region Value collaboration and learning Seek to expand professional networks Show initiative and innovation Are willing to complete self-directed learning Have a clear vision for their future contributions to peacebuilding Programme Impact Since its creation, the fellowship has helped develop a growing network of peacebuilding leaders throughout Asia and the Pacific. Participants often go on to: Lead peace initiatives Influence policy reforms Conduct impactful research Strengthen civil society organizations Facilitate community dialogue Build regional partnerships The fellowship serves as a platform for long-term leadership development and collaboration. Application Timeline Applications Open 10 June 2026 Applications Close 12 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early and prepare supporting materials well before the deadline. APPLY HERE Why You Should Apply The William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding is one of the most prestigious opportunities available for emerging peacebuilders in Asia and the Pacific. The fellowship offers far more than professional training. It provides: International exposure Leadership development Global networking opportunities Access to leading experts Financial support for professional growth Long-term membership in a regional peacebuilding network For professionals dedicated to promoting peace, dialogue, and conflict transformation, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to strengthen their leadership journey and amplify their impact. Click HERE for more information about the William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026. Conclusion The 2026 William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding represents an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders working to build more peaceful and inclusive societies across Asia and the Pacific. Through international exchanges, leadership development, mentorship, networking opportunities, and financial support, the fellowship equips participants with the tools needed to address complex peace and conflict challenges in their communities. As societies across the region navigate increasingly complex social and political dynamics, the need for innovative and collaborative peacebuilders has never been greater. This fellowship offers a powerful platform for rising leaders to expand their influence, strengthen their expertise, and contribute meaningfully to lasting peace. Discover more global Fellowship and Asian opportunities on OFY. The post William P. Fuller Fellowship in Peacebuilding 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Emerging Peacebuilders Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Security and Technology (Digital Tools) in Geneva/Remote
summary: The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career professionals and recent graduates through its Graduate Professional Programme (GPP) 2026. This position focuses on Security and Technology, specifically supporting Digital Tools such as the Cyber Policy Portal and Artificial Intelligence Policy Portal. Based in Geneva, Switzerland, with remote participation options available, this 6-month role provides a structured entry point into international peace and security work within the United Nations system. The programme is designed for individuals passionate about emerging technologies, digital governance, cybersecurity policy, and global disarmament issues. The Graduate Professional will work under the UNIDIR Security and Technology Programme, contributing directly to research support systems, communication tools, and policy knowledge platforms used by international stakeholders. About UNIDIR and Its Mission The United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) is an autonomous UN institute funded voluntarily and based in Geneva. It plays a critical role in: Producing independent research on disarmament and global security Facilitating international dialogue on emerging security threats Supporting policy development on technological risks in security environments Promoting innovative solutions to global peace and stability challenges The Security and Technology Programme focuses on understanding how technological advancements impact international security, including: Artificial intelligence governance Cybersecurity policy frameworks Digital transformation in security systems Emerging technologies and global risk mitigation Position Overview: Graduate Professional (Security and Technology – Digital Tools) This role is designed to support the development, maintenance, and improvement of UNIDIR’s digital knowledge platforms, including: Cyber Policy Portal Artificial Intelligence Policy Portal These platforms serve as key global resources for policymakers, researchers, and institutions working in international security and technology governance. Key Position Details Position Title: Graduate Professional (Security and Technology – Digital Tools) Organization: United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) Location: Geneva, Switzerland or Remote Duration: 6 months (with possible extension) Employment Type: Full-time (100% occupational rate) Start Date: Negotiable, as soon as possible Closing Date: 15 June 2026 Stipend: Remote: USD 750 per month In-person Geneva: USD 1500 per month Core Responsibilities The selected Graduate Professional will support both technical and policy-related functions. Responsibilities include: Digital Tools and Platform Support Assist in maintaining and updating UNIDIR’s digital policy portals Support troubleshooting and rapid resolution of technical issues Contribute to improving platform usability and functionality Research and Communication Support Draft official communications, reports, and documentation Assist in preparing policy briefs and analytical summaries Support synthesis of research into accessible formats Content Development and Outreach Create infographics and communication materials Support promotional activities for digital tools Contribute to internal and external communication strategies Coordination and Administrative Support Participate in meetings, workshops, and conferences when required Provide written and verbal briefings to teams Support day-to-day coordination and administrative tasks Required Skills and Competencies This role requires a combination of technical literacy, analytical thinking, and communication skills. Core Skills Strong research and analytical abilities Excellent writing and editing skills Ability to summarize complex information into clear outputs Project management and organizational capability Strong interpersonal and teamwork skills Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Desired Interests Candidates should demonstrate interest in: International peace and security Disarmament policy Artificial intelligence and emerging technologies United Nations system and global governance UN Core Competencies Applicants are expected to demonstrate: Professionalism: High standards of integrity and responsibility Communication: Clear and audience-adapted messaging Teamwork: Collaboration across diverse cultural environments Planning and Organization: Efficient time and task management Client Orientation: Responsiveness to stakeholder needs Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this Graduate Professional Programme, applicants must meet one of the following: Academic Requirements Currently enrolled in a graduate programme (second university degree or higher), OR Have completed a graduate degree within the last two years Preference is given to candidates with backgrounds in: International Relations Political Science International Law International Security Technology Policy or related fields Experience Requirements No strict professional experience required Prior research experience is considered an advantage Interest in AI, cybersecurity, or emerging technologies is highly desirable Strong digital literacy is required Language Requirements Fluency in English (written and spoken) is mandatory Knowledge of another UN language is an advantage Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package including: Cover letter Inspira Personal History Profile (PHP) Short responses to screening questions (maximum 30 words each) Application Format Instructions All documents must be combined into a single PDF file File name format: Sectec_SURNAME_Name_PHP-CL Email submission required to: unidir.recruitment@un.org Subject line: UNIDIR Security and Technology June 2026 GPP Application Important Notes CVs are not accepted in place of a PHP Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered Women are strongly encouraged to apply Applicants must indicate preference for remote or in-person participation Medical insurance coverage is required for participation Screening Questions Overview Applicants must address the following: Eligibility based on current or recent graduate status English fluency and communication ability Research experience and analytical capability Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools and related software Ability to manage and coordinate multiple tasks effectively Deadline The final deadline for application submission is 15 June 2026 (midnight Geneva time). Late submissions will not be accepted. Why This Opportunity Matters This Graduate Professional role offers a unique entry point into the United Nations system, particularly in one of the fastest-growing policy areas: the intersection of security and emerging technology. Participants will gain: Direct exposure to UN policy development processes Experience working on global digital security tools Practical skills in research, communication, and digital platforms Professional experience within an international organization Networking opportunities in global security and technology governance For more information, visit the official website. Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post UNIDIR Graduate Professional Programme 2026: Security and Technology (Digital Tools) in Geneva/Remote appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand (Fully Funded Fellowship Available)
summary: Applications are now open for the United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026, a prestigious training programme organized by the Codification Division of the United Nations Office of Legal Affairs (OLA) in cooperation with the Government of the Kingdom of Thailand and the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP). This highly respected programme offers legal professionals from across the Asia-Pacific region a unique opportunity to deepen their knowledge of international law, engage with leading experts and practitioners, and strengthen their capacity to contribute to international legal processes and institutions. The course will take place in Bangkok, Thailand, from 23 November to 16 December 2026 and will be conducted entirely in English. For professionals working in government institutions, academia, international organizations, non-governmental organizations, and legal practice, the United Nations Regional Course in International Law represents one of the most valuable capacity-building opportunities in the field of international law. The programme accommodates up to 26 participants and includes a limited number of fully funded fellowships as well as spaces for self-funded participants. About the United Nations Regional Course in International Law The United Nations Regional Course in International Law is part of the broader United Nations Programme of Assistance in the Teaching, Study, Dissemination and Wider Appreciation of International Law. The programme was established to strengthen understanding of international law among legal professionals and government officials while promoting the peaceful settlement of disputes and international cooperation. Through intensive lectures, discussions, and practical learning opportunities, participants gain advanced knowledge of international legal principles and contemporary issues affecting the international community. The Regional Course serves as an important platform for legal professionals across the Asia-Pacific region to exchange experiences, build networks, and enhance their expertise in international law. Host Institutions The 2026 Regional Course is organized through a collaboration between: United Nations Office of Legal Affairs (OLA) Codification Division of the United Nations Government of the Kingdom of Thailand United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) The course will be hosted at ESCAP facilities in Bangkok, Thailand. Course Dates and Location Location Bangkok, Thailand Venue United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) Programme Dates 23 November – 16 December 2026 Language of Instruction English Number of Participants Up to 26 participants The programme will include: Fellowship recipients Self-funded participants Objectives of the Programme The United Nations Regional Course in International Law aims to: Strengthen participants’ knowledge of international law. Enhance understanding of contemporary legal issues. Promote the teaching and dissemination of international law. Build professional capacity among legal practitioners and policymakers. Encourage international cooperation through legal frameworks. Foster regional and international professional networks. The course is specifically designed for professionals who are already working in fields related to international law and wish to expand their expertise. What Participants Will Learn Participants will receive intensive training covering key areas of international law and related legal disciplines. The programme typically addresses: Public International Law Sources of international law State responsibility International legal obligations Treaty law International Organizations United Nations system Functions of international institutions Global governance mechanisms International Dispute Settlement Peaceful settlement of disputes International courts and tribunals Arbitration mechanisms Human Rights Law International human rights frameworks Treaty bodies Monitoring mechanisms International Humanitarian Law Laws of armed conflict Protection of civilians Humanitarian obligations International Economic Law Trade law Investment law Economic cooperation Emerging Legal Challenges Climate change law Cyber law International security Sustainable development Participants benefit from interactions with distinguished academics, judges, diplomats, legal practitioners, and United Nations experts. Fellowship Benefits A limited number of selected participants will receive United Nations fellowships. The fellowship covers: International Travel Economy-class airfare to and from Bangkok. Accommodation Accommodation provided for the duration of the programme. Medical Insurance Comprehensive medical insurance coverage during the course period. Training Materials All official course materials. Living Stipend Fellowship recipients receive a modest stipend designed to cover: Food Local transportation Daily living expenses The stipend follows United Nations fellowship regulations and reflects local living costs. Accessibility Support Reasonable accommodation may be provided upon request for participants with disabilities. Self-Funded Participation The Regional Course also accepts a limited number of self-funded participants. Self-funded participants enjoy: Access to all lectures and activities. Participation in the same programme. Equal academic standing. The same certificate upon successful completion. However, self-funded participants are responsible for: International travel. Accommodation. Medical insurance. Living expenses. Training materials are provided to all participants regardless of funding status. Eligibility Criteria To be considered, applicants must meet the following requirements. Legal Background Applicants must possess: A legal education background. Professional Experience Candidates must have: Professional experience in the field of international law. The programme is intended for professionals rather than students. Language Requirement Applicants must be: Fluent in English. As the entire programme will be conducted in English, participants should be able to actively engage in lectures and discussions. Availability Selected participants must commit to attending the entire course. Partial attendance is not permitted. Who Can Apply? The programme is open to candidates from: United Nations Member States in the Asia-Pacific region. The observer State to the United Nations in the Asia-Pacific region. Eligible applicants may come from: Government Institutions Ministries Foreign affairs departments Legal departments Judicial institutions Academic Institutions Universities Law faculties Research institutes International Organizations Intergovernmental organizations Regional organizations Civil Society Organizations NGOs working on legal and policy issues Legal Professionals Lawyers Legal advisers Policy experts Current United Nations staff members are not eligible to apply. Selection Process Applications are reviewed through a competitive selection process. The United Nations considers: Professional Qualifications Relevant legal education and expertise. Scope of Professional Responsibilities The extent to which international law forms part of the applicant s work. Relevance of Training How participation will contribute to the applicant’s professional responsibilities. Gender Balance The programme promotes gender equality, and female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. Important FAQs Can I Apply If I Am a Student? No. The Regional Course is specifically intended for professionals with practical experience in international law. Can I Reapply If I Was Previously Unsuccessful? Yes. Applicants who were not selected in previous years may submit a new application. Can I Attend Only Part of the Course? No. Selected participants must attend the programme in its entirety. Can Several Candidates Apply From the Same Country? Yes. Countries are encouraged to nominate multiple qualified candidates. Does the United Nations Charge Any Application Fee? No. The United Nations does not charge any fees at any stage of the application process. Applicants should never provide banking information during the application process. Click HERE for more FAQ s Why You Should Apply This programme provides a rare opportunity to: Receive advanced training from UN experts. Expand your international law expertise. Build professional networks across the Asia-Pacific region. Strengthen your career in diplomacy, international organizations, government service, academia, or legal practice. Experience a prestigious United Nations educational programme. Gain a deeper understanding of contemporary international legal challenges. For professionals seeking to advance their careers in international law, this course offers exceptional value and recognition. Application Deadline Applications for the 2026 Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific are open until: 29 June 2026 Late submissions may not be considered. How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the Official Application Form and submit all required documentation to the Codification Division of the United Nations Office of Legal Affairs. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review: Programme brochure Application form Eligibility requirements Frequently Asked Questions before submitting their applications. Contact Information For additional inquiries, applicants may contact: Codification Division, United Nations Office of Legal Affairs Email: rcil-asia@un.org Visit HERE to read more about the United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand. Conclusion The United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 is one of the most prestigious professional training opportunities available for legal professionals in the region. Through intensive instruction, engagement with leading experts, and access to a global network of practitioners, participants will strengthen their understanding of international law while contributing to the promotion of legal cooperation and the rule of law worldwide. With fully funded fellowship opportunities available, this programme provides an exceptional platform for legal professionals seeking to expand their expertise and advance their careers within international legal and diplomatic spheres. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE . The post United Nations Regional Course in International Law for Asia-Pacific 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand (Fully Funded Fellowship Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
